title: Sens. Moran, Klobuchar, Ernst, Cortez Masto Introduce Securing Americaâ€™s Fuels Act
content: U.S. Senators Jerry Moran (R-Kan.), Amy Klobuchar (D-Minn.), Joni Ernst (R-Iowa) and Catherine Cortez Masto (D-Nev.) introduced the bipartisan Securing Americaâ€™s Fuels (SAF) Act to strengthen the sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) industry, provide new markets for farmers and lower emissions in the transportation sector. This legislation would reinstate the SAF bonus credit that would allow qualifying SAF producers to receive up to $0.35 or $1.75 per gallon, and it would extend the 45Z Clean Fuel Production Tax Credit for all clean fuels through 2033. â€œKansas has a long history as a leader in agriculture and aviation, and this legislation will strengthen these industries while bolstering U.S. energy innovation,â€ said Sen. Moran. â€œAs a founding member of the Senate Sustainable Aviation Caucus, I am committed to supporting the sustainable fuels industry to bolster our aviation industry, open new markets for Kansas farmers and rural communities and promote American energy independence." â€œDomestically produced biofuels strengthen our energy independence, support our farmers, and boost rural economies,â€ said Sen. Klobuchar. â€œThe introduction of the Securing America's Fuels Act is an important step as we work to restore the full value of the 45Z Clean Fuel Production Credit. By extending the credit to 2033 and restoring the creditâ€™s value to $1.75 this legislation gives farmers and biofuel producers the certainty they need to develop sustainable aviation fuel and other low carbon fuels.â€
author: Analysts Account
